questions.q: In which year was the Indian National Congress founded?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885.

questions.q: Which retired British civil servant is closely associated with the founding of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Allan Octavian Hume, a retired British civil servant, played a key role in founding the Congress.

questions.q: In which city was the first session of the Indian National Congress held in 1885?
questions.explanation: The first session of the Congress was held in Bombay in December 1885.

questions.q: What became the central recurring forum of the Indian National Congress for nationalist debate?
questions.explanation: The Congress's annual sessions became the central forum for nationalist debate and demands.

questions.q: The Indian National Congress grew into the principal organization of what movement?
questions.explanation: The Congress became the principal organization of the Indian independence movement.

questions.q: Who was the first President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Womesh Chandra (W. C.) Bonnerjee presided over the first Congress session in 1885.

questions.q: The Indian National Congress is often referred to by which common abbreviation?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress is commonly abbreviated as the INC.

questions.q: In its early decades, the Congress chiefly used which kind of method to press its demands?
questions.explanation: The early Congress relied on petitions, resolutions, and constitutional appeals to the British government.

questions.q: Which leader, regarded as the Father of the Nation, became the dominant figure in the Congress from around 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi became the leading figure of the Congress and the freedom movement from around 1920.

questions.q: The Indian National Congress led India's struggle against rule by which colonial power?
questions.explanation: The Congress led the struggle for independence from British colonial rule.

questions.q: At which 1929 session did the Congress adopt the resolution for Purna Swaraj, or complete independence?
questions.explanation: At the 1929 Lahore session the Congress adopted the resolution for complete independence.

questions.q: The 1907 split of the Congress into Moderates and Extremists occurred at which session?
questions.explanation: The Congress split into Moderates and Extremists at the 1907 Surat session.

questions.q: Who became the first woman President of the Indian National Congress, in 1917?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant became the first woman President of the Congress in 1917.

questions.q: Who was the first Indian woman to be elected President of the Indian National Congress, in 1925?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu became the first Indian woman to be elected President of the Congress, in 1925.

questions.q: At which 1924 session did Mahatma Gandhi serve as Congress President, the only session he presided over?
questions.explanation: Gandhi presided over the Congress only once, at the Belgaum session of 1924.

questions.q: The Congress launched which mass movement in August 1942 with the call 'Do or Die'?
questions.explanation: The Congress launched the Quit India Movement in August 1942 with Gandhi's call to 'Do or Die.'

questions.q: At which 1916 session did the Moderates and Extremists reunite within the Congress?
questions.explanation: The two factions reunited at the Lucknow session of 1916, which also produced the Lucknow Pact.

questions.q: Subhas Chandra Bose was elected Congress President in 1938 and 1939 at sessions held where, before he resigned?
questions.explanation: Bose was elected President at Haripura in 1938 and Tripuri in 1939 before resigning after a dispute with Gandhi.

questions.q: Badruddin Tyabji, who presided over the 1887 Congress session, was notable as the organization's first what?
questions.explanation: Badruddin Tyabji became the first Muslim President of the Congress in 1887.

questions.q: Who delivered the presidential address at the 1929 Lahore session that adopted Purna Swaraj?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru presided over the 1929 Lahore session that adopted the goal of complete independence.

questions.q: The famous Congress resolution declaring 'Fundamental Rights and Economic Policy' was passed at which 1931 session?
questions.explanation: The 1931 Karachi session passed the resolution on Fundamental Rights and the National Economic Programme.

questions.q: What was the original stated aim of the early Congress in its relationship with British rule?
options: Reform and a greater Indian voice within the existing system
Union with Britain as an equal nation
questions.explanation: The early Congress sought reforms and a larger Indian role within the colonial system rather than its overthrow.

questions.q: The 1916 Lucknow Pact was an agreement between the Congress and which other organization?
questions.explanation: The Lucknow Pact of 1916 was a cooperation agreement between the Congress and the All-India Muslim League.

questions.q: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad served a long unbroken term as Congress President spanning which crucial years?
questions.explanation: Azad served as Congress President from 1940 to 1946, covering the Quit India and pre-independence years.

questions.q: The 1936 and 1937 Congress sessions, which adopted a socialist-leaning programme under Nehru, were held at Lucknow and which other town?
questions.explanation: After Lucknow in 1936, the Congress met at Faizpur in 1937, its first session held in a rural village.

questions.q: At its 1885 founding session, how many delegates approximately attended the first Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: The first Congress session in 1885 was attended by about 72 delegates.

questions.q: The Congress Working Committee, its key executive body, is informally known by what nickname?
options: The High Command / Cabinet of the Congress
questions.explanation: The Congress Working Committee functioned as the party's executive 'High Command' or cabinet.

questions.q: Which 1924 development saw C. R. Das and Motilal Nehru form a group within the Congress to contest legislative council elections?
questions.explanation: Das and Motilal Nehru founded the Swaraj Party to enter the legislative councils, working within the Congress fold.

questions.q: The Congress Socialist Party, a left-wing group formed within the Congress in 1934, was led by figures including which leader?
questions.explanation: Jayaprakash Narayan was a founding leader of the Congress Socialist Party formed in 1934.

questions.q: Womesh Chandra Bonnerjee, the first Congress President, belonged to which profession that dominated the early Congress leadership?
questions.explanation: Bonnerjee was a Calcutta barrister, typical of the lawyer-dominated early Congress leadership.
